perturbation.original_text: A. PPG SHANGHAI desires DISTRIBUTOR to market and sell PPG's "Deltron" and "ACS" brand products, as hereinafter defined in Section 2 (the "Products"), to the repair chain shops in P.R.China as listed on Appendix 4 (the "Territory");
perturbation.changed_text: A. PPG SHANGHAI desires DISTRIBUTOR to market and sell PPG's products to the repair chain shops in P.R.China as listed on Appendix 4 (the "Territory");
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'as hereinafter defined in Section 2 (the "Products")', the text now lacks a clear definition of 'Products'. While 'Products' is still referenced, its scope is now ambiguous, because Section 2 isn't linked as the source of defintion. This creates a contradiction since Section 2 attempts to define the products, but the new text doesn't see that Section 2 is where this defintion should be found.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. PRODUCTS 2.1 The Products and subject matter of this Agreement shall be the products listed below manufactured and sold by PPG SHANGHAI or affiliated companies of PPG SHANGHAI. Additional Products may be added to or deleted from the list at the sole discretion of PPG SHANGHAI: (a) Global Deltron Refinish Products. (b) ACS Products. (c) Ancilliaries confirmed in writing for distribution by PPG SHANGHAI. (d) Mixing equipment and related accessories as approved and supplied by PPG SHANGHAI. (e) Any other products as PPG SHANGHAI may launch from time to time, at its absolute discretion. 2.2 Specific product codes for above category a, b, c, d, and e shall reference PPG SHANGHAI Price List in effect as of January 1, 2005 on Appendix 5.
perturbation.justification: The original text linked Section 2 as the source of definition for 'Products'. The changed text omits this link, making the definition of 'Products' ambiguous in Section A. This directly contradicts Section 2, which attempts to define 'Products' for the agreement, because Section A no longer explicitly acknowledges or relies on this definition. This omission creates an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language (the reference to Section 2) was removed. The removal of that essential language alters the meaning of Section A, in relation to the meaning of Section 2, thereby causing contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: PPG SHANGHAI shall provide DISTRIBUTOR Quarterly and Annual Rebate as listed in Appendix 3 if agreed targets are achieved on time.
perturbation.changed_text: PPG SHANGHAI may consider providing DISTRIBUTOR rebates if targets are achieved.
perturbation.explanation: Changing from 'shall provide...as listed in Appendix 3' to 'may consider providing' removes the mandatory nature of the rebates and the reference to Appendix 3, where the specific rebate structure would be detailed. Section 9(g) and 4.3 still mention agreed sales targets. This creates an internal contradiction because 9(g) and 4.3 references an Appendix 3 that determines rebates upon meeting these targets, but the current section 8(e) says rebates is at PPG SHANGHAI's discression, making those targets possibly irrelevant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DISTRIBUTOR agrees that DISTRIBUTOR shall, use all reasonable efforts , do the following: (a) provide its customers with services regarding the Products, including the safety and toxicological aspects of Products handling and ensure that delivery to customers is made of technical information provided by PPG SHANGHAI regarding Products, including the safety precautions and toxicological aspects of Products handling; (b) maintain an adequate supply of Products to expedite customer deliveries and give prompt and efficient service to its customers in the Territory; (c) maintain knowledge of the market in the Territory and regularly communicate such knowledge to PPG SHANGHAI; (d) be responsible that the labels for the Products meet all governmental and all applicable laws of the Territory regulatory requirements and comply with all applicable laws of the Territory; (e) provide to its customers, Product technical support and training and ensure its customers maintain technical support and training to end-users. (f) plan and develop promotional and advertising strategies to enhance PPG SHANGHAI's image and sales value in the region. (g) Achieve agreed and signed sales target (Appendix 3).
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated PPG SHANGHAI to provide rebates as per Appendix 3 upon achieving targets. The changed text makes the provision of rebates discretionary ('may consider providing'), which contradicts the 'Achieve agreed and signed sales target (Appendix 3)' in the contradicted text. Since the distributor is obliged to meet the targets outlined in Appendix 3, it expects the rebates outlined in Appendix 3 to be mandatorily applied. The change introduces uncertainty and makes the effort required to achieve the sales target potentially unrewarded, resulting in a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: PPG SHANGHAI and DISTRIBUTOR will follow PPG's Responsible Care(R) Distributor Guidelines as set forth in the attached Appendix I (Responsible Care(R) is a registered trademark in the United States of the American Chemistry Council ). Accordingly, PPG SHANGHAI will furnish certain information to DISTRIBUTOR regarding product safety and handling aspects of the Products, and DISTRIBUTOR, in addition to its independent responsibility to obtain and implement a product safety program regarding the Products, will implement and conform to PPG SHANGHAI's product safety recommendations and Responsible Care(R) Distributor Guidelines, DISTRIBUTOR will also provide its customers, employees and other third parties foreseeably exposed to the Products with appropriate warnings, advice and other material regarding the Products, including all product safety and handling material provided by PPG SHANGHAI, and will ensure that the Products are used, stored, handled, distributed, transported and disposed of in a manner consistent with all of the above recommendations.
perturbation.changed_text: PPG SHANGHAI and DISTRIBUTOR will promote product safety. Accordingly, PPG SHANGHAI will furnish certain information to DISTRIBUTOR regarding product safety and handling aspects of the Products, and DISTRIBUTOR will provide its customers, employees and other third parties foreseeably exposed to the Products with appropriate warnings, advice and other material regarding the Products, including all product safety and handling material provided by PPG SHANGHAI, and will ensure that the Products are used, stored, handled, distributed, transported and disposed of in a manner consistent with all of the above recommendations.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'PPG's Responsible Care(R) Distributor Guidelines as set forth in the attached Appendix I' and the distributor's obligation to 'implement and conform' to those guidelines creates an in-text contradiction. While the Distributor is still required to follow safety recommendation, the specific guidelines, found on Appendix I are no longer being reffered. Since the rest of this section still mandates certain safety actions, the omission of Appendix I and its binding nature creates uncertainty about what specific guidelines the distributor must adhere to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PPG SHANGHAI and DISTRIBUTOR will promote product safety. Accordingly, PPG SHANGHAI will furnish certain information to DISTRIBUTOR regarding product safety and handling aspects of the Products, and DISTRIBUTOR will provide its customers, employees and other third parties foreseeably exposed to the Products with appropriate warnings, advice and other material regarding the Products, including all product safety and handling material provided by PPG SHANGHAI, and will ensure that the Products are used, stored, handled, distributed, transported and disposed of in a manner consistent with all of the above recommendations.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that PPG SHANGHAI and DISTRIBUTOR will follow 'PPG's Responsible Care(R) Distributor Guidelines as set forth in the attached Appendix I' and that the distributor will 'implement and conform' to these guidelines. The changed text removes this specific requirement and reference to Appendix I. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to safety actions with the original implied understanding that they would adhere to Appendix I's guidelines. By removing the direct reference and obligation to conform to Appendix I, the change creates uncertainty and a potential conflict in how safety measures are implemented, violating the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as the essential language referencing Appendix I and the binding nature of guidelines found within is removed.
